name: Source Fidelity review for Neuroendocrine And Systemic Signal Reset bundle
summary: The staged source, study, finding, and outlook records are source-faithful for revision 1. PubMed metadata and abstracts support the TRIIM, oxytocin, hypothalamic IKK-beta/NF-kappaB/GnRH, and hypothalamic stem-cell exosome records; the ClinicalTrials.gov API record supports TRIIM-X as recruiting with no posted results in the current record; and the Nature Aging primary article supports the aged-rhesus low-dose klotho cognition finding. The staged records preserve the key human, animal, nonhuman-primate, endpoint, duration, and no-results boundaries.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged TRIIM-X record correctly treats ClinicalTrials.gov status as no-results program context, not evidence of benefit or safety.
findings.why_it_matters: The registry is time-sensitive and can lag the real study, so source-fidelity depends on preserving the as-of boundary and not treating recruitment or estimated completion as outcome evidence.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the current no-results caveat and re-check the registry before publication if publication is delayed materially.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged klotho source uses the primary open-access Nature Aging article and DOI rather than the PubMed-indexed short commentary returned by a nearby search result.
findings.why_it_matters: Using the primary article is necessary because the staged finding relies on study design, dose, sample, and endpoint details not available from the commentary record.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the manual source ID and primary DOI/URL; do not replace it with the commentary PMID during publication.
